title: Practice Set 3 — End-to-End Pipeline Optimization
description: Optimize NordGrid’s 59-pipeline platform: critical path analysis, caching for shared DataFrames, materialized view strategy, DAG review, and execution architecture redesign.

datasets.description: 59-pipeline DAG: 12 Bronze, 20 Silver, 20 Gold, 7 Platinum. Dependencies, durations, consumers, SLA tiers.

tasks.title: Identify the Critical Path in NordGrid’s Pipeline DAG

description.context: NordGrid’s daily pipeline fleet runs at 06:00. The SLA requires all Gold tables ready by 07:00 (60-minute window). With 59 pipelines across 4 layers, end-to-end time depends on the critical path.
description.task: Examine the DAG structure. Determine the critical path, calculate duration, assess SLA risk, evaluate which optimization has most impact.
description.validationHint: The critical path is the longest Bronze-Silver-Gold chain. Platinum (SLA=low) is excluded from Gold SLA analysis.

evidence.content:
Chain A: PL-007 (bronze 15 min) > PL-025 (silver 30 min) > PL-048 (gold 18 min) = 63 min
Chain B: PL-003 (bronze 12 min) > PL-019 (silver 28 min) > PL-041 (gold 20 min) = 60 min
Chain C: PL-011 (bronze 14 min) > PL-031 (silver 25 min) > PL-055 (platinum 90 min) = 129 min (SLA=low)

fields.label: Which chain is the critical path for Gold SLA?
options: Chain A (63 min) - longest Gold chain, exceeds 60-min SLA
Chain B (60 min) - exactly at SLA boundary
Chain C (129 min) - longest total chain
fields.correctAnswer: Chain A (63 min) - longest Gold chain, exceeds 60-min SLA

fields.label: Which pipeline yields the most SLA benefit if optimized?
options: PL-025 (silver, 30 min) - longest on critical path, most optimization potential
fields.correctAnswer: PL-025 (silver, 30 min) - longest on critical path, most optimization potential

fields.label: An engineer optimizes PL-041 (Chain B) from 20 to 10 min. Does this help Gold SLA?
options: No - PL-041 is on Chain B not Chain A. Chain A still takes 63 min.
Yes - brings Chain B under SLA
fields.correctAnswer: No - PL-041 is on Chain B not Chain A. Chain A still takes 63 min.

tasks.title: Evaluate Caching Strategy for Shared Silver DataFrames

description.context: 11 Silver tables are read by 3+ Gold pipelines. Top table: 8 GB, read 6 times daily (45s each Parquet read, 3s cached).
description.task: Evaluate caching for top shared tables. Determine break-even, memory impact, and caching policy.
description.validationHint: First read populates cache (full cost). Subsequent reads save I/O time.

evidence.content:
silver.readings_se_20: 8 GB, 6 reads/day, 45s Parquet, 3s cached
silver.readings_fi_03: 6 GB, 6 reads/day, 35s Parquet, 2s cached
silver.readings_fi_06: 7 GB, 5 reads/day, 40s Parquet, 3s cached
Available cache: 192 GB. Top 3 = 21 GB (11%).

fields.label: Daily savings from caching silver.readings_se_20?
options: 210s - first read populates cache, 5 subsequent save 42s each
fields.correctAnswer: 210s - first read populates cache, 5 subsequent save 42s each

fields.label: Cache all 5 shared tables (35 GB) or top 3 (21 GB)?
options: All 5 - 18% of cache budget is safe
Top 3 - 80% of savings at 60% memory cost, reserve rest for ad-hoc
fields.correctAnswer: Top 3 - 80% of savings at 60% memory cost, reserve rest for ad-hoc
fields.secondaryCorrectAnswer: All 5 - 18% of cache budget is safe

fields.label: When to unpersist cached tables?
options: After last downstream consumer reads it
fields.correctAnswer: After last downstream consumer reads it

fields.label: Key caching principle?
options: Cache where (reads x time_savings) > memory_cost - value proportional to read frequency x savings
fields.correctAnswer: Cache where (reads x time_savings) > memory_cost - value proportional to read frequency x savings

tasks.title: Design Materialized View Strategy for Gold Tables

description.context: 8 Gold pipelines differ in freshness (30 min to 24h) and read frequency (2-40/day). Some are MV candidates.
description.task: Identify MV candidates vs pipeline-only tables. Design refresh schedule for the best candidate.
description.validationHint: Best MV candidates: high reads + relaxed freshness. Worst: low reads or strict freshness.

evidence.content:
gold_billing: 18 min compute, 12 reads/day, <30 min stale
gold_regional: 8 min, 25 reads/day, 2h stale OK
gold_compliance: 15 min, 3 reads, <30 min stale
gold_ops_summary: 5 min, 40 reads/day, 1h stale OK
gold_exec_report: 7 min, 2 reads, 24h stale OK

fields.label: Best MV candidates?
options: gold_regional (25 reads, 2h stale) + gold_ops_summary (40 reads, 1h stale)
fields.correctAnswer: gold_regional (25 reads, 2h stale) + gold_ops_summary (40 reads, 1h stale)

fields.label: Which should remain as pipelines (not MVs)?
options: gold_billing + gold_compliance - <30 min freshness means refresh overhead exceeds savings
fields.correctAnswer: gold_billing + gold_compliance - <30 min freshness means refresh overhead exceeds savings

fields.label: Refresh schedule for gold_ops_summary (40 reads/day, 1h stale OK, 5 min compute)?
options: Every 30 min (net +40 min cost vs no MV)
Every 60 min (net 80 min savings vs no MV)
fields.correctAnswer: Every 60 min (net 80 min savings vs no MV)
fields.secondaryCorrectAnswer: Every 30 min (net +40 min cost vs no MV)

tasks.title: Review Pipeline DAG for Optimization Opportunities

description.context: 5 proposed optimizations. One has no Gold SLA effect. Must rank valid ones by impact.
description.task: Evaluate each proposal, identify the ineffective one, rank the rest.
description.validationHint: Optimizing non-critical-path or non-Gold-SLA pipelines has zero Gold SLA impact.

evidence.content:
Opt 1: Cache shared Silver (saves 210s across reads)
Opt 2: Fix PL-025 join (saves 12 min ON critical path)
Opt 3: Parallelize two Gold pipelines (saves 18 min)
Opt 4: Optimize PL-055 Platinum SLA=low (saves 30 min)
Opt 5: Merge 4 small Gold pipelines (eliminate 3 redundant reads)

fields.label: Which has NO Gold SLA effect?
options: Opt 4 (Platinum SLA=low) - not on Gold critical path
fields.correctAnswer: Opt 4 (Platinum SLA=low) - not on Gold critical path

fields.label: Highest Gold SLA impact?
options: Opt 2 (fix join, 12 min ON critical path)
fields.correctAnswer: Opt 2 (fix join, 12 min ON critical path)

fields.label: Rank 4 valid optimizations by impact (highest first)

description.context: CTO targets: 60-min Gold SLA, 20% compute reduction, Platinum isolation. Current: 63 min critical path, 1078 min total compute.
description.task: Design execution architecture: pipeline order, caching, MVs, resource pools. Produce config artifact.
description.validationHint: Fix critical path first. Add caching for shared tables. Isolate Platinum from Gold.

evidence.content: Gold SLA: 60 min. Compute: reduce 20% (1078 to 862 min). Platinum must not delay Gold. No new hardware.

fields.label: Highest-priority change for Gold SLA?
options: Fix critical path: optimize PL-025 join (30>18 min), Chain A drops from 63 to 51 min
fields.correctAnswer: Fix critical path: optimize PL-025 join (30>18 min), Chain A drops from 63 to 51 min

fields.label: How to isolate Platinum from Gold?
options: Separate resource pools: Gold 80% during 06-07, Platinum 20% then full access after
fields.correctAnswer: Separate resource pools: Gold 80% during 06-07, Platinum 20% then full access after

starterScaffold.content:
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, lit

spark = SparkSession.builder.appName('nordgrid-exec-arch').master('local[*]').getOrCreate()
df_dag = spark.read.csv('PX3_data_pipeline_dag.csv', header=True, inferSchema=True)

# YOUR SOLUTION BELOW
# Produce execution architecture DataFrame.
# Columns: pipeline_id, pipeline_name, layer, execution_wave (1-5),
#   resource_pool ('critical'/'standard'/'ml'),
#   cache_output (yes/no), materialized_view (yes/no),
#   optimization_applied (description)
# Store in 'architecture'



# YOUR SOLUTION ABOVE

# Verification
print('Execution Architecture:')
architecture.groupBy('layer', 'resource_pool').count().orderBy('layer').show()
print(f'Cached: {architecture.filter(col("cache_output") == "yes").count()}')
print(f'Total: {architecture.count()}')

assertions.check: all(c in architecture.columns for c in ['pipeline_id', 'layer', 'execution_wave', 'resource_pool'])

synthesisNotes.rationale: Combines every optimization lever into a single execution architecture for 59 pipelines under SLA, cost, and isolation constraints.

selfCheck.scaleMatters: Critical path, caching, MV decisions require 59-pipeline DAG. Not meaningful at single-pipeline scale.
selfCheck.theoryPracticeBoundary: Theory teaches optimization levers individually. Practice applies all simultaneously under competing constraints.
